title: Shared definitions for blemees/3 wire frames
description: Reusable $defs referenced by the per-frame schemas. Not a frame schema itself.

SessionId.description: Client-owned session identifier, opaque to the daemon. Distinct from the agent's own `native_session_id` (the ACP sessionId), which the daemon reports back on session.opened when it differs.

RequestId.description: Opaque correlation id set by the client on a request frame; echoed on the matching reply.

Seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number the daemon injects on every session-stream frame (those routed through Session.on_event).

PeerPid.description: Peer PID captured via SO_PEERCRED; may be absent where the platform doesn't expose it.

ProfileName.description: Profile name. `default` is synthesised; others come from the config file or over-wire profile.create (#25).

AttentionReason.description: Why a session entered needs_attention (#24, §6). The notify `test` reason is emitted only on notify.test_result, never as a session attention edge.

MessageContent.description: A user turn: a plain string, or an array of ACP content blocks. The daemon currently forwards text blocks to the agent and rejects non-text blocks (invalid_message).

AgentSpec.description: One agent's config inside a profile spec. `agent_command` (or `command`) is the ACP binary; the rest are optional knobs applied after session/new.

ProfileSpec.description: An over-wire profile definition (#25). Agent shape, most specific first: an `agents` table (multi-agent), a single `agent` object (the `default` agent), or flat agent fields directly on the object. `name` may live here or at the frame's top level.

Notification.description: The notify payload (#24, §6) — the body POSTed to a webhook and the shape carried in status.attention[] and notify.test_result.notification.

PermissionOption.description: One choice in a relayed permission request, mirrored from ACP PermissionOption.
